title: Chicken Kyiv Pasta
summary:
In Partnership With Aldi. #ad

Air fryer crispy chicken combined with garlicky spaghetti. This low-effort recipe has all the flavours of a chicken kyiv. Plus, twenty cloves of garlic. Yes, twenty.

method.description: Lay a chicken breast between two sheets of baking parchment or cling film. Bash with a rolling pin until about 3mm thick. Repeat with the second breast.
Tip the flour onto a tray and season with salt and pepper. Crack the eggs onto another tray, whisk, and season. On a third tray, mix the panko and seeds with salt and pepper.
Dip each chicken breast into the flour, then the egg, then the seeded breadcrumb mix, pressing to coat fully.
Place the chicken in your air fryer basket and drizzle with olive oil. Air-fry at 200°C for 6–8 mins per side, or until golden and cooked through. The internal temp should read 74°C.
While the chicken cooks, bash the garlic cloves with a rolling pin — no need to peel, the skins will be easier to remove after this.
Place a wide, deep pan over low heat and coat the base with olive oil. Add the garlic cloves and cook gently for 10 mins, stirring occasionally.
Pour in the spaghetti and chicken stock. Simmer for 12 mins, or until the stock is absorbed and the spaghetti is cooked al dente.
Stir in the butter, lemon zest, and chopped parsley. Taste and season with salt and pepper. Plate up the garlicky pasta with your crispy chicken on top and a wedge of lemon on the side.

tips: Best served fresh for maximum crispiness.
The pasta will keep for up to 3 days in the fridge.
Chicken can be reheated in the air fryer at 180°C for 5 mins.
Not freezer-friendly due to the breaded coating and fresh garlic.
Mixed seeds → Just sesame or skip for plain breadcrumbs
Panko → Crushed cornflakes or regular breadcrumbs

chefs.name: Ayala Daly
chefs.summary: Ayala Daly is Mob's content producer and resident vibe bringer. You’ve probably seen her all over the stories but she’s no stranger to the kitchen. Ayala blends her background as a food researcher with her passion for cooking. With a knack for experimentation, she crafts easy-to-follow recipes bursting with bold flavours, drawing inspiration from regular trips to her favourite farms and the thrill of all things spicy.
